reviews.review_text: Playing Detroit in 2026 is wild because it went from cool sci-fi game to  basically a documentary. We're only 12 years away from 2038 now, and all that stuff in the game people losing jobs to automation, protests against tech companies, and humans getting way too emotionally attached to AI is literally happening outside our windows. We don't have Connor-level androids walking around just yet, but with how fast AI and robotics are moving right now, CyberLife doesn't even feel that unrealistic anymore.

For me, Detroit: Become Human stands at the very top of the decision-based / choice-driven genre. In terms of how decisions actually impact the story and outcomes, I don’t think there is anything better out there.

The story is extremely unique and powerful, especially with the moral questions it raises throughout the game. It constantly makes you think about your choices and their consequences, which is exactly what this genre is all about.

The three main characters are all written in a very interesting and distinct way. Each of them has their own perspective, motivation, and emotional depth, which makes switching between them feel fresh and engaging every time. Even though there weren’t a huge number of shocking plot twists, the game still managed to stay exciting and immersive from start to finish.

Overall, it’s an absolute must-play and a real gem in gaming. Everyone who enjoys story-driven games should experience it at least once.

Unfortunately, I did run into a few crashes and bugs during my playthrough. Also, sometimes it felt like my character said something slightly different than what I expected based on the dialogue choices, which was a bit confusing and occasionally frustrating.

But despite those issues, the strengths of the game easily outweigh the negatives.

8.7 / 10

Amazing story, memorable characters, and meaningful choices. Every decision feels important and can completely change the outcome of the game.
Connor is the GOAT.
Definitely worth playing.
an amazing interactive movie, as always from Quantic Dream. it's perfect and will keep you hooked 100% for a couple of days

Detroit: Become Human exceeded my expectations. If you enjoy movie style games where you face moral dilemmas and your decisions have a real impact on the future of the characters and the overall story, you will probably have a great time with this game. The story takes place in 2038 in the city of Detroit, where androids have taken over jobs that humans no longer want to do. People are allowed to live more comfortable lives with more time for entertainment. At first, everything seems like normal technological progress and a natural part of society, but over time tensions between humans and androids begin to surface. The game gradually builds momentum as some androids start developing deviations, which allow them to feel emotions and make decisions based on their own beliefs. The atmosphere also plays a huge role, especially because you slowly build emotional connections with many of the characters.

Players follow the stories of three androids, each with a completely different perspective on the world. Connor is an android designed to assist the police with murder investigations and the hunt for deviants, Kara works as a housekeeper and nanny for a young girl named Alice and Marcus takes care of an elderly artist named Carl, who treats him not as a machine, but more like a real person and a member of the family. Each character has a unique storyline, different struggles, and choices that can significantly affect the rest of the game. The game rarely feels repetitive and constantly makes you think about which decisions are right and what consequences they may bring.

---

9/10

Detroit: Become Human is one of those games that makes you realize how little your choices matter in most other "choice-driven" games.

The most impressive thing about Detroit is the sheer number of possible outcomes. From the very beginning, you're constantly making decisions, and the game remembers almost all of them. What starts as a few simple choices eventually grows into an enormous branching story where relationships, character fates, entire storylines, and even major endings can change depending on what you did hours earlier.

What fascinated me most was seeing the flowcharts after each chapter. Sometimes I would finish a section and realize I had only seen a small fraction of the possible paths. The amount of content hidden behind different decisions is honestly incredible. This is one of the few games where replaying it can genuinely feel like playing a different story rather than simply choosing a different ending.

My biggest recommendation is: don't Google anything.

Don't search for the best ending. Don't look up the correct dialogue option. Don't try to optimize every decision. The game is at its best when you're making choices based on your own feelings and instincts rather than trying to achieve a perfect outcome.

In fact, you'll probably make mistakes. You'll lose characters. You'll regret certain decisions. You may even end up with a bad ending. But that's exactly what makes the experience memorable. The beauty of Detroit is that you make choices without knowing their consequences, just like people do in real life. When something goes wrong, it feels like your mistake. When something goes right, it feels earned.

Could I replay the game and get a better outcome? Absolutely. But I think my first playthrough, with all its imperfect decisions and unexpected consequences, was far more valuable than following a guide ever could have been.

If you enjoy story-driven games where your decisions genuinely matter, Detroit: Become Human is one of the best examples of the genre.
good game, i always enjoy option based game like walking dead from telltale and i like this game

All I have to say is that this game is still absolutely peak, even after 6 years. The graphics are excellent, the storyline is goated, and it's a great way to challenge and refresh your way of thinking. One of the best parts is how many different choices and outcomes there are, allowing you to create your own unique story and discover countless plot twists along the way. Overall, I give it a 10/10.

This game absolutely blew me away… I was not ready to be this emotionally invested and bawl my eyes out, but here we go. I felt responsible for every character, every decision and every outcome as every choice can have a huge impact how the story goes (or ends) And the amount of choices, branches and endings are honestly crazy, the replay value of this game is insane! Meanwhile with other kinds of game you can replay it doesn't change that much of the story, with D:BH a different branch could mean a whole new story and a brand new experience - you really write the story as you go.

It being a 8 year old game, the graphics are still immaculate, everything looks so real and beautiful, the cameramoves at certain scenes make it feel like you are watching a movie. I've always been afraid of games with QTE and found them a bit too stressful but I seriously enjoyed them in here, they gave me the right amount of adrenaline and fit well with the action scenes. (Also you can choose your difficulty.)

Detroit: Become Human is the first narrative-driven, your choice matter game I've played and honestly, the bar is very high now for me in this genre. All in all, it's one of the most if not the most impactful games I've ever played, I HIGHLY recommend it to anyone and everyone!

(I have more hours in it on Epic, where I first bought the game, but there's no achievements there - and I want achievements lol - and I knew I wanted to replay anyway, so I bought on Steam too)
What a ride. Amazing film making. Writing could've been better but WHAT A RIDE. Absolutely loved it.

Where do I even start, the storyline, the acting, the music, the dialogs, ... Everything about this game is perfect and I LOVE it. I love every minute I spent playing it. Kara, Connor, Markus, All had a rich story where you wouldn't feel one being less at all. The game environment and the design is magnificent, clothes, buildings & all. Valorie Curry (Kara), Bryan Dechart (Connor) & Jesse Williams (Markus) were the best actor choices, all put their hearts and souls into this. David Cage wrote a masterpiece. i could sit here and write for pages how much I love and enjoyed this game, but you should go on and play it yourself. For me, after 44 hours of playing this piece of art and getting all the achievements, this game is now in my heart and memory for ever.

♥♥♥

Amazing story with tons of choices its actually insane your story truly is yours, also it does a perfect job of showing the consequences of developing an intelligent lifeform then putting restrictions on it and expecting no retaliation.
getting this game at 80% off almost feels criminal. this is absolute CINEMA. can’t wait for Quantic Dream to drop a new game, their storylines are always peak.

This has to be one of the greatest games ever made. Easily in my top 3 games of all time, if not higher. Not once did Detroit: Become Human disappoint me with its choices or storytelling. I genuinely had multiple heart attacks playing through some scenes because every decision felt important and stressful in the best way possible.  The combat and quick-time events somehow made me more stressed than most online shooter games, and the dialogue combined with the soundtrack creates such an intense atmosphere the entire way through. Every character felt memorable, and what happened with Chloe at the end completely caught me off guard. Markus will always be my GOAT. He went from having absolutely everything and being the happiest android imaginable to losing everything he had, and somehow still fighting his way back to having it all again. I thought Connor was a bum at first, but I’m really glad the game gave me the choice to change that over time.
A masterpiece from start to finish. 10/10.

“ and on the other side of the ocean... is freedom. 
That’s what I always believed (...) 
those enemies on the other side of here...
 if we kill them all, does that mean we’ll be free? ” - Eren Yeager
Amazing game but it will run completely fine and crash for no reason so many times that it ruins the experience alot

Detroit: Become Human isn't a casual game, it's a master-piece. It makes you consider your decisions and asks the hard-hitting questions about what humanity really is. The storyline alone is worth every cent and second you'll spend in the game. 

On the more technical side, the graphics still hold-up in the big '26 and the gameplay is really smooth. It runs really well on Steam Deck and fairly well on my laptop. 

 If you're concerned about replay value, there are so many different paths and combinations of decisions that will change everything! I've played through about four times now fully and still haven't gotten every ending, but it still leaves me feeling like I just learned a huge lesson about myself.

A very good game, one of my favorites. I've played it many times and it surprises me every time. The characters and story are perfectly written. I return to it time and time again after finishing it. I love that you can create your own unique story. I recommend it.

Beautiful door diversity that many games should look up to, but I must point out one major flaw...
This game sets horrible door beauty standards that may cause some game doors to feel not good enough. There should be a disclaimer at the start of the game stating any door standards set by this game should not have to be met by others.

-5 points without the disclaimer

Detroit: Become Human is basically an interactive sci-fi movie, but it’s one of the few games in that genre where your choices genuinely feel like they matter.
The story follows three androids with very different perspectives, and the game constantly throws difficult decisions at you that can completely change relationships, major events, and even who survives until the end.

What makes the game stand out is just how many branching paths and outcomes there are. Characters can die early, entire storylines can change dramatically, and replaying chapters to see different outcomes is surprisingly interesting. The flowchart system showing all the possible choices and paths was honestly a genius addition.

Visually, the game is also incredibly impressive. Facial animations, environments, and character performances are all excellent, and some scenes genuinely feel cinematic in the best possible way.

The good:
- Choices actually matter
- Excellent presentation and visuals
- Strong performances from the cast
- Multiple endings and branching story paths
- Connor’s storyline is fantastic
- Tense and emotional decision-making
- Replayability is much higher than expected
- Flowchart system encourages experimentation
- Soundtrack fits the atmosphere perfectly
- Some scenes are incredibly memorable

The bad:
- Gameplay is very limited outside of choices and QTEs
- Some story themes are handled a bit awkwardly
- Certain plot twists are predictable
- Markus’ storyline is less interesting than Connor’s
- Heavy reliance on quick-time events
- Dialogue can occasionally feel overly dramatic
- Some choices feel misleading
- If you dislike cinematic games, this probably won’t change your mind

Overall, Detroit: Become Human is one of the best interactive narrative games out there. It’s emotional, cinematic, and surprisingly replayable thanks to how many different outcomes and story paths exist.

[h1] David Cage was right........ [/h1] 

This game isn’t just imagination. Detroit: Become Human feels like it’s based on real life and the future we might actually face. In the future, it’s very possible that androids will take over a lot of human jobs.

This isn’t just something from a game like Detroit: Become Human because it’s already happening in real life. We can already see self checkout machines replacing cashiers and robots working in factories. Because of that, a lot of people are starting to worry that humans won’t be needed anymore or could become unemployed. Honestly, I’m scared of that what will happen in next future.

But at the same time, I don’t think the world will change as fast as games make it look. Humans will still be needed, just in different kinds of jobs.

[h1] About story game (my opinion) [/h1] 
The truth is, robots don’t actually “want” to replace humans. They don’t have feelings, emotions, or desires. They only do what humans program them to do. So the real danger isn’t robots suddenly turning against us. It’s more about how humans choose to use this technology, and whether companies care more about profit than the people losing their jobs and becoming unemployed.

Even with all this progress, humans still have things that are hard to replace real emotions, life experiences, relationships, creativity, and the way we understand each other. Android can copy or imitate these things, but it can’t truly feel them.

[h1] He saw the future [/h1] 
About David Cage… maybe he actually had a point. Even though he makes story games, the ideas in his work feel closer to reality than people think. It’s not just about games anymore it’s about a fking where the world could be heading.

A lot of people today are already afraid of becoming unemployed because machines are becoming smarter, especially younger people trying to find work. That’s why this game feels so real right now.

[h1] Let’s hope, brothers and sisters. [/h1] 
hopefully, the future won’t turn out exactly like the game. Hopefully, technology will stay as a tool that helps humans instead of replacing everything. And hopefully, everyone will still have the chance to work (unemployed), live comfortably, and exist alongside all these changes.

Great one of a kind game, must play.
Use a controller for best experience.

Phenomenal storytelling, I found this game by watching others play it and I think I've seen around 7 different alternate endings; each of them packing a real gut-punch. I've yet to sit down and play through more than one iteration of the game but I cannot recommend this game enough.

[h2]A masterpiece. My Score: 10/10[/h2]

This game fits the current rise of AI and our societal transformation. There is no better game that fits our mindset, and how the world could look like with androids in the near future. Detroit Become Human (DBH) covers so many relatable topics in our real world. The line between reality and sci-fi is so thin, and yet it is a fundamentally an amazing game.

[h3]The pros:[/h3]

[list]
[*]The art direction and world design are superb. The futuristic Detroit feels almost real, a sense of that is how it could really look like. It's baffling. The design blows my mind. 
[*]The user interface is great
[*]The game offers so many choices and paths that a re-run of certain chapters or a new game is a must in order to see all game content
[*]The characters are amazing! I love the relationship between Hank and Connor. Kara and the little one. There are a handful of people that I truly cared and was interested in!!! 
[*]The story is simply over the top that deserves at least 10x Oscars. There are only a few, a very few games I care for the story. 
[*]DBH is most likely a game genre that you're not familiar with. It feels and plays like an interactive story. You decide the fate of the movie character so to speak. However it is done in a  way that will capture you and the controls are very easy. The game is very accessible and simple
[*]The overall quality of the game is impeccable
[*]Good acting and voice over
[*]A handful of paths and decision making that allows for multiple endings based on your actions. Take the word "multiple" literally because it really has that many endings and variations! 
[/list]

[h3]The cons:[/h3]

[list]
[*]The camera can be funky and clunky at times
[*]During combat, the player is focused too much to press the right buttons. The consequence is, that all the action or what's happening live on screen or background cannot be fully appreciated. It feels different because due to the game style and mechanics, you're not 100% in control of the action itself. You just set the path and trigger the right actions for your character. Maybe in future games the combat mechanic could be more fluid. 
[/list]

[h3]Neutral:
I would've love so much to keep Kara's long hair[/h3]

I consider DBH to be one of those games that you MUST play in your lifetime. It is really THAT good. Take a pause from your regular shooter, RPG, adventure games and try this one out because you wont regret it! Those who have played HEAVY RAIN before or games from Supermassive Games, will feel very familiar. I played this game back to back on Playstation and bought it on Steam as well. DBH sold 12 million copies so far. The record and success speaks for itself.

They actually made a choices matter game where your choices matter. Even though I enjoy Telltale games for the most part, this absolutely destroys them in terms of interactivity, choices mattering & potential to choose how the characters act. Had a lot of fun on my first playthrough. It does slightly lose it's edge on repeat playthroughs though, especially for the Kara storyline as that one is bizarrely linear and repetitive compared to the other two. This game also stumbles at times with some odd logic & clumsy comparisons to real historical racism/segregation.

its such a beautiful game crafted so perfectly i love the graphics and the story i love connor i feel like im just like connor i hope connor always gets to live a happy and free life i want to play again just so i can see connor one more time

Best interactive movie game I've ever played.
Beautiful graphics, emotional story and three great characters. Your choices really matter. I loved it. 10/10

*some spoilers* 7/10 for its technical achievement for the time and quality of the acting/voice acting. The first half of the game was an easy 9/10 or more as it was generally done very well. But the game quickly devolved into being just about "civil rights" retreading events we already solved in the USA decades ago. All the cool background stuff like the world threat of war, mass unemployment, etc ... all of that was nothing and never addressed in the actual narrative. RA9, Chloe as the first sentient Android ... none of this was explained. It was tossed away as a nothing burger or left to nonsensical allegory. Hank's past was left only to "dead son". Bad guys were mustache-swirling Nazi-like villains. There was just a whole lot of bad narrative design during the 2nd half of the game that really sullied the overall story. Especially since they never planned for a sequel. The "best" ending was brief and empty, feeling like the game ended several chapters before it should have. It just left me feeling unsatisfied in the end and that is a dramatic bummer for me.
My name is Connor, the android sent by steam to give this game a perfect review. 10/10.

A cinematic story with (too many) quick-time events to move forward. You decide over the fate of a dozen androids, and a few humans, while they attempt to figure out if they are alive..

It took me about 11 hours to complete.

+ Graphics, music and design are very well done.
+ Voice acting is both excellent and emotional.
+ Interesting and thought-provoking story.
+ Nice little details in the menu.
+ Appears like some big choices are possible, unlike a lot of other Quantic Dream games.
+ Some actual morally ambiguous choices.

+/- Not a big fan of having time-pressure in an detective type game, but it's handled well.

- It drags on a little bit.
- The quick-time events get tedious.
- Everything that can go wrong...

It's a somewhat hesitant thumbs up. The total experience is great, but some of the moment-to-moment gameplay is a bit tedious.

fantastic game, fantastic price of $40
easy recommendation!

The "AI vs. Human" trope isn't new, but this game makes it personal. It powerfully captures the raw, historical struggle of oppressed communities fighting for their basic humanity. Combined with mind-blowing facial capture technology, gorgeous lighting, and a deeply detailed near-future Detroit, the emotional stakes feel incredibly real. A brilliant narrative experience that stays with you long after the credits roll.

Absolute cinema. This is not only a game but a deep philosophical exploration of the boundaries of artificial intelligence in society and the foundational ethics of mass-producing sentient beings solely meant for menial labor. It's a story intended to make you really think about what it means to be human.

Incredible storytelling where every choice actually matters, re-playability value is insane. Graphics are beautiful. I played this back when I had it on PS4 and playing it again in 2026 is wild because I can actually see this happening in the near future with the insane advancements being made in technology and AI. Very Black Mirror-esque, if you like that show then you will love this game. 

"28 STAB WOUNDS" of emotional damage. 10/10 masterpiece. <3

Hello all acyberlife androids and humans. This game is like a fun thing where you can be androuid and also you can be evil android who kill human. my mommy she buy that for me becuase my step dad beat me and she say is sorry and asked me what i want. very fun game

The story is good, and it's also very interesting (a couple of inconcistencies aside).

The problem is that this "game" barely has any game in it. The only gamey mechanic is the environment scanning. Everything else could as well just be a visual novel or an interactive movie. This is not a problem per se, but if the "game" expects you to replay it multiple times and then also strips away the possibility to skip cutscenes (there's not even a mod that does that), it just becomes a waiting game.

When replaying, it was very clear that whenever a scene is completely new, it's interesting, whereas if you played it even just once, it's boring as hell. Essentially, it's really good as long as you play it only once and also don't want a "game" but rather a story.

After being convinced to play this game by my friend, I can definitely say that this game is incredible. I played through the pacifist route and only ended up killing 2 people (Connor and Luther) but after reseting the chapters I saved everyone. The story was amazing to follow even though I thought 3 people was weird at first, I could remember each character story. Connors story (being my favorite) was awesome and I loved his interactions with Hank. Karas story made me grow attached to her and Alice which is a sign of a good timeline of events. Markus's story stressed me out in a healthy way with the choices I had to make. Overall 11/10 and now one of my favorites!
Very unique and immersive. Infinitely replayable
Played a whole game,now im suspiciouse that my airfryer might betray me
Out of the games i played by Quantic Dream the best was Beyond two Souls, then Detroit: become human, then Heavy rain, there all 10/10 but i just fell like Beyond was the best IMO

i don't even know what to say. such a beautiful and humane creation. it seems closer to our current reality and i love the way it makes me think and question about life, about the world, humanity, anout myself as well. i'm still recovering from the not-entirely-good ending i've got but i will replay this again for sure. kudos to everyone involved in this game. what a masterpiece.

tough to get my wife to like a game. I played the first level with her nearby and she was hooked, demanding a new save file for herself to start right away. This game has terrific pacing, allowing players to check in and out as if short episodes of a TV show were directed to have complete mini stories that weave together to tell a bigger story. I absolutely adore the branching pathways that you can compare to others using worldwide data.

If you play games for the experience and the story, this game is a must-play. I was deeply moved by the plot and the characters. Every character felt memorable and well-developed, making the journey even more impactful.

took me 15 hrs to do this once. but there is so much more playthroughs to be done. this includes the platinum for this game. its a bit overwhelming at first if you started off wanting all the trophys but play the game however u want and replay it as many times in order to get all acheivements. 7.5/10
An amazing game where your actions shape the story. Grate characters. I loved it

Detroit: Become Human is easily one of my favourite games of all time. Every choice feels like it matters, which is great because I spent half the game overthinking every decision like my life depended on it. The story is gripping, the characters are unforgettable, and the sheer number of endings makes it endlessly replayable (I'm currently on my third playthrough at time of review). Connor went from 'cool detective android' to 'must protect at all costs' in record time. It's cinematic, beautiful, emotional, and devastating at times I cried actual tears during my first playthrough and fully sobbed at some parts. It's also stressful in the best way possible, and proof that pressing the "wrong" choice can haunt you for days. 
10/10

The story was great, the controls were good, the choices were challenging and satisfying, but in some places there were unnecessary interactions — for example, having to press a button just to get Alice off the boat felt pointless. I'm not sure if it was because I played on Experienced difficulty, but I would have preferred player inputs to only happen during truly important moments or during fights. Other than that, I had a lot of fun and got emotional while playing.

Certifed Banger. The game treats the characters like actual people and throws some real questions at you about freedom and identity without getting boring and repeating stuff. The world feels believable too, and the way the stories connect adds a lot of weight.
Absolute cinema. Great story, and there’s heaps of choices that actually make you stop and think instead of just rushing through everything.
got the worst ending possible in my first and only playthrough, need to play it a second time ngl. very unique and amazing stories.
I've never played choice-based games before, but I must say this one is exceptional so far! It's basically like a movie...that you're playing? Best way to describe it
i already played this game on ps4 back then, then i bought it again on steam still an 10/10 even more cus there's no limitation on quality of the game

[h1] Absolut cinema [/h1]
It's a very beautiful game with a good storyline.
An interesting revelation of Theseus' paradox
However, there was a problem with the sound, which suddenly disappeared, which had a negative impact on the immersion (possibly due to my devices).
[h3]Must-pass [/h3]
[h3]9/10 [/h3]

Detroit: Become Human is straight-up one of the coolest story games I’ve ever played. Like, from the first minutes it just hits different — you’re thrown into this futuristic world with androids, and every choice you make actually changes stuff. One bad decision and everything goes to ♥♥♥♥. It keeps you hooked af. The characters are just top tier. Connor is lowkey my favorite, but Markus and Kara are also awesome, although Kara's storyline might be boring sometimes. The graphics look real asf and it really fits the atmosphere of the game, and the music just hits hard at the right moments. Honestly, this game is just vibes. If you like story games where your choices matter and you can play it your own way, you have to try it, cause I've tried a few choice-based games and this is my favorite. In this game the choices you make really matter and change the whole story, not like the minor changes in Telltale games. Long story short, this game is just peak.
It's a good interactive story. If you want action or adventure, this is not the game.
I remember watching gameplays for this on youtube back when it was released, but actually playing it now in the era on AI is kinda crazy. Anyways this is an awesome game!
Excellent game with a great story and cinematic experience. Runs well with my setup and didn't experience any bugs or glitches.
This game is so well made that not even words can describe it. Life changing experience.

Even after replaying this game two years later, I was still amazed by how well it holds up. The choices feel meaningful, the story branches in many different ways, and the consequences of your actions can completely change the outcome.
I enjoyed it so much that I tried to complete multiple playthroughs just to see as many endings as possible. Few games make me want to replay them over and over, but Detroit: Become Human does exactly that.
The characters are memorable, and the game gives you plenty of time to build relationships with them, making every important decision feel impactful. Whether things go well or terribly wrong, it always feels like the result of your own choices.
I wish there were more games like this. If you enjoy story-driven games where your decisions truly matter, this is an easy recommendation.

Probably the first and last interactive story I will ever play. Besides that, this game is very entertaining, and you can and will get engaged.

Conners storyline is my personnel favorite.
Incredibly unrealistic fantasy game. The idea of a working class guy owning a home with a backyard in 2038 is pure fantasy. God I love data centers.

Amazing story, memorable characters, and meaningful choices. Every decision feels important and can completely change the outcome of the game.
Connor is the GOAT.
Definitely worth playing.
9/10
it's one of those butterfly effect games with multiple endings but this game has multipleeeeee endings to it. amazing overall and especially in the time we're currently in for AI.

I had watched a gameplay from a YouTuber in the past, and I decided to purchase the game when it was on discount and have a try as well. Thankfully, I do not remember much from the gameplay which means that it did not spoil my experience with the game.

Having played the game for 4 hours, I can say that I like it even though I have not finished it yet. Detroit contains stunning graphics, audio and all of the Android stories are fabulous. On the other hand, what I do not like about the game is that it has some jump scares, which I believe make the game less fun, as this is not a horror video game. 

As far as the technical aspect of the game is concerned, the game runs smoothly on my computer in max settings (I have the specs attached in the case one wants to see them). However, I am not pleased with the controls using keyboard and mouse because I need to make fast actions sometimes, and it asks me to press keys or move the mouse in a direction, and I end up messing the combos up. I think that if someone plays the game with a controller, it would be more controllable. 

All in all, I love Detroit, and I cannot wait to explore the areas in the story that I haven't explored yet.

Not my kind off game, i can tell its extremely well done but for me its a 6,5 not cause it bad but cause i like games like darktide and titanfall 2. Shooters not story games. Would still recomend tho.
Fun interactive game, every choice matters and gives a good message

This is one of the best story games ever and very well put together. The thing about this game is that every choice really does matter, either in the long run or right away. It forces you to make quick choices in stressful situations that may seem easy at first, but then it turns out wrong later. The gameplay and story come together perfectly and it is very interesting to see all of the characters in their own situations and grow throughout the game. I plan on replaying the game and choosing things I didn't in the first run to see what would be different. This game is no doubt worth every single penny.
Just finished the game. Its absolutely fantastic and I really recommend playing

Fantastic story, memorable characters, and choices that actually matter. Every time I thought I knew where things were going, the game surprised me. Definitely worth playing, especially if you enjoy narrative-driven games.

First time playing, i hated because of how slow it is, and then as the time went, i realised why this game had amazing feedback, definetly a game every gamer should play, the amount of choices this game gives us is truly overwhelming.

I loved this game, this is the first 3A game I bought. No regrets, enjoyed unlocking the storylines and nurturing relationships with the characters!

Very entertaining story. It took a little bit to get to the really good parts but the build up and implications set by the story progression of each character kept me coming back for more. Also, this game will put you into an existential crisis. Have fun.

5/5 – A Masterpiece of Choice-Driven Storytelling

Detroit: Become Human is one of the greatest narrative games I have ever played. From the very first chapter, I was completely invested in the lives of Connor, Markus, and Kara. Every decision felt meaningful, and knowing that my choices could completely change the story kept me on the edge of my seat the entire time.

The graphics are stunning, the motion capture is incredible, and the voice acting brings every character to life. What impressed me most was how the game made me think about morality, freedom, humanity, and the consequences of my actions. Few games have made me feel so emotionally connected to their characters.

Connor's storyline was absolutely unforgettable, Markus's journey was inspiring, and Kara's story was heartbreaking and beautiful at the same time. The branching paths and multiple endings gave me a reason to replay the game and discover new outcomes. The game is widely praised for its deep choice system, multiple endings, and emotional storytelling, which are exactly the elements that made it so memorable for me.

Detroit: Become Human is more than just a game—it's an experience. It made me laugh, stress, hope, and even tear up. If you enjoy story-driven games where your choices truly matter, this is an absolute must-play.

A masterpiece from start to finish. Highly recommended!

Absolutely THE single best game I've ever played in my entire time on this planet as a video game lover. In 2026, a game that I was able to be solely invested in, and completely captivated by every step of the way. This game was truly beyond its time, and has some extremely deep and philosophical gameplay. I mean it's truly a spiritual experience. Reading the article in the laundromat actually moved me to tears in a way no video game has ever touched on before in my life. Completely unexpected reaction to that article. Highly recommend giving it a read while you're in there btw. But yeah, overall, it felt like my decisions really had weight to them, and anything could result in something wildly different than what I'd hoped for. Do yourself a favor and buy this game YESTERDAY if you already haven't.

10/10

This game is an absolute masterpiece.

The graphics are gorgeous, the characters look incredible, the animations are top-tier, and somehow the game still looks better than a lot of titles released years later.

But the real star of the show is the story.

Every decision matters. Every choice can completely change what happens next, who lives, who dies, who trusts you, and ultimately which ending you’ll get. And that’s the best part: there isn’t just one ending.

There are dozens of different paths, outcomes, and consequences, which makes every playthrough feel unique. I constantly found myself wondering:

“What would’ve happened if I had chosen something else?”

And before I knew it, I was replaying entire chapters just to see how differently things could unfold.

The three protagonists are fantastic, the world is believable, and the game does an amazing job making you genuinely care about the characters.

Another thing that surprised me was how well optimized it is.

I originally played and finished it on a GTX 1650 laptop model, and despite not having the most powerful GPU in the world, I was comfortably getting well over 100 FPS while the game still looked absolutely stunning.

Seriously, more developers should study whatever black magic optimization was used here.

Beautiful graphics.

Excellent optimization.

This is one of those rare games that stays in your head long after the credits roll.

Easy recommendation.

One of the most greatest games of all time. The story and the characters are simply a work of art created by pure masterminds. This entire storyline is intriguing with the QTES and the cool movement patters you have to do. Its crazy how every single little detail can and will affect your story and how every little branch can stem from one decision. This game was simply a wonderful experience and I believe that everyone should try this game at least once during their lifetime.

From the technical point of view, my review should have been actually positive, if we skip the usual "poor porting from consoles" and "issues with optimisation on PC" part and agree this has become a distinctive QD flavour rather than a bug.

The scenario branching alone and the number of possible outcomes deserve praise. But I do strongly believe that Quantic Dream will only produce their first actual masterpiece when David Cage agrees to move as far away from the scenario writing or lore creating as possible.

This game could have been an interactive competitor to Black Mirror. However, David Cage's habit - using banal parallels and moralising to squeeze as many tears from the audience as he can - has ruined literally everything. The scenario and lore are helpless against the most basic analysis. 

Watch an episode of Black Mirror and notice the single thing their scenario writers don't resort to - and which Cage uses too damn often.

Moreover, intended or not, somehow Cage managed to do the same thing the "carbon footprint" PR inventors once did to consumers - focusing the guilt on the inherent violence of humankind, which is hardly a surprise to anyone, while burying the real culprit (the egghead who endows a tool with sentience without warning anyone) in its hardest-to-reach ending.

P.S. And, by the way, the "androids" in this game aren't really androids. They are humans... playing the roles of androids rather poorly.
